title: Begonia nevadensis
taxonomy: Begonia nevadensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Begoniaceae. The genus Begonia is a large and diverse group of plants, with over 1,900 species. Begonia nevadensis is native to the mountainous regions of Nevada, USA. It is a member of the Begonia sect. Begoniella. The classification of Begonia nevadensis is further divided into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, species, which are Plantae, Magnoliophyta, Magnoliopsida, Cucurbitales, Begoniaceae, Begonia, and Begonia nevadensis respectively.
uses: Begonia nevadensis is a species of Begonia found in Nevada, USA, however the information on its medicinal uses is limited. It is primarily known for its ornamental value and is not well-documented in terms of its traditional or modern medicinal applications. parts: Begonia nevadensis is a species of flowering plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico.
